flashcards.front: What are the 4 values of the Agile Manifesto?
flashcards.back:
1) Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
2) Working software over comprehensive documentation
3) Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
4) Responding to change over following a plan

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Agile and Waterfall methodology?
flashcards.back: Waterfall is sequential and rigid with phases flowing downwards; Agile is iterative, flexible, and embraces changes throughout the development cycle.

flashcards.front: What are the 12 principles behind the Agile Manifesto?
flashcards.back: Customer satisfaction through early and continuous delivery, welcome changing requirements, deliver working software frequently, business and developers work together daily, build projects around motivated individuals, face-to-face conversation, working software is the primary measure of progress, sustainable development, continuous attention to technical excellence, simplicity, self-organizing teams, regular reflection and adjustment.

flashcards.front: What is a Sprint in Scrum?
flashcards.back: A time-boxed iteration (usually 2-4 weeks) during which a potentially releasable product increment is created.
An ordered list of everything that is known to be needed in the product, owned by the Product Owner and continuously refined.
The set of Product Backlog items selected for the Sprint plus a plan for delivering them, created during Sprint Planning.

flashcards.front: What are the three roles in Scrum?
flashcards.back:
1) Product Owner - defines features, prioritizes backlog
2) Scrum Master - facilitates the process, removes impediments
3) Development Team - cross-functional members who deliver increment
A measure of the amount of work a team can complete in a Sprint, typically measured in story points.
A short, simple description of a feature from the end-user's perspective in the format: 'As a [role], I want [goal] so that [benefit].'
A graphical representation showing work remaining versus time, helping teams track progress and predict completion.
A visual workflow management method using a board with columns (To Do, In Progress, Done) to limit work in progress and optimize flow.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of Daily Stand-up meeting?
flashcards.back: A brief daily meeting where team members share what they did yesterday, plan to do today, and identify any blockers.
A meeting held at the end of each Sprint where the team reflects on what went well, what didn't, and how to improve.
A shared understanding of what it means for work to be complete, ensuring quality and transparency across the team.

flashcards.front: What is Incremental Delivery in Agile?
flashcards.back: Delivering working software in small, usable increments rather than waiting for the entire project to be complete.
